title: palicourea subscandens
intro: Palicourea subscandens is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the "wild coffee" due to its resemblance to coffee plants. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Palicourea subscandens include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
